title: Police Check an Email About Money Help
summary: Police are looking into an email from Lord Mandelson to Jeffrey Epstein about a big money help plan from the European Union after a money crash in 2010. The email talked about a €500 billion bailout to save the Euro. Lord Mandelson is a former ambassador, and Jeffrey Epstein was a convicted sex offender. The police want to know if sharing this email was a crime. Another person, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, is also being checked for possibly sharing secret papers with Epstein. The police are checking if these actions broke the law. Lord Mandelson says he did not do anything wrong and is helping the police. He did not want to talk more about it. The email was found in files from the US Justice Department about Epstein.

background_read: This article is about a police investigation into an email from Lord Mandelson to Jeffrey Epstein. In 2010, after a big financial crash, the European Union planned a €500 billion bailout to help save the Euro. An email suggests Lord Mandelson told Epstein about this plan before it was announced. Jeffrey Epstein was a convicted sex offender, and sharing such information might be illegal. Lord Mandelson is a former UK ambassador to the US, and he says he did nothing wrong. The police are checking if this was a crime, and they are also looking at another person, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, for similar reasons. This helps us understand how police work to keep laws safe.
Article_Structure: WHO: Lord Mandelson, Jeffrey Epstein, police officers, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor
WHAT: An email about a €500 billion EU bailout shared before announcement
WHERE: United Kingdom and European Union
WHY: To investigate if sharing the email was a criminal offence

perspectives.description: The police are investigating to see if laws were broken by sharing secret information.
He believes he did not act criminally and is cooperating with the investigation.
